claims: The skill requests Edit, WebSearch, and WebFetch permissions that are excessive for its primary purpose of conducting multi-dimensional reviews of local Claude Code skills. While Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, and Write (for generating reports) are reasonable for reading files, running validation scripts, and outputting results, Edit permission could allow unintended modification of the skills under review, contradicting the assessment-only goal. WebSearch and WebFetch are not core to reviewing local skill structure, content, quality, usability, and integration, and their inclusion expands the attack surface unnecessarily, posing potential privacy and security risks without clear necessity.
